text.title: Mutation Score on Changed Files Only
text.summary: Scope mutation testing to the diff and break the CI when the score falls under the threshold.

section.title: Run mutation only on the diff
section.p: Full-suite mutation runs are too slow for per-PR CI. Configure the runner (Stryker, mutmut, PIT) with incremental mode and feed it only the files that changed in the PR. The CI fails when the score on the diff falls below the break threshold, regardless of overall project score.

rule.statement: CI MUST run mutation testing scoped to changed files on every agent-authored PR with `break: 70`, `low: 60`, `high: 80` thresholds. The job MUST fail (non-zero exit) when the diff mutation score falls below `break`. Coverage percent alone MUST NOT be used as the merge gate when an LLM authored or modified the tests.
rule.rationale: Coverage measures execution; mutation measures detection. AI-written tests routinely show high coverage with low mutation score because assertions are weak ("not None", "len > 0"). Mutation surfaces every weak assertion as a concrete surviving mutant.

example.note: Only changed files mutated; CI cost stays bounded.

example.code: # Coverage-only gate; AI agents cheat through it - run: pytest --cov=src --cov-fail-under=90
example.note: 90% coverage with weak assertions still passes; mutants survive.

antipattern.description: Lowering the break threshold whenever a PR fails it.
antipattern.reason: The threshold ratchet is the gate; relaxing it on red converts mutation testing into theatre.

section.reference: Mutation testing keeps coding agents honest.
